meaning: n.<日>歌舞伎(创始于17世纪的日本传统剧种)
examples: The language of kabuki is difficult to understand, but earphones are provided to explain the story, and there are also earphone services available in English.
The kabuki actors wear traditional costumes, and sing in addition to acting.
Because of the strange costumes and reversed-gender roles, Okuni’s performances came to be known as kabuki dance.
Another speciality of the Kabuki stage is a footbridge (hanamichi) that extends into the audience.
Kabuki plays are about historical events and moral conflict in relationships of the heart.
